openQuestions.text: Should fallback-router.ts be wired into real GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback now, or remain an offline/reference-only primitive for this phase?
Which bidirectional path couplings between deep-loop-workflows, deep-loop-runtime, commands, agents, docs, tests, and scripts must be repaired mechanically during the merge?
What reference migration is required across commands, agents, READMEs, skill docs, feature catalogs, manual testing playbooks, specs, and advisor corpus entries?
Which system-spec-kit tooling should be borrowed, wrapped, or left external instead of duplicated inside the merged deep-loop system?
Does the proposed system-deep-loop structural layout preserve the current workflow/runtime split without creating ambiguous ownership or broken relative paths?

resolvedQuestions.question: Are bidirectional path-coupling repair rules complete after the move?
resolvedQuestions.answer: The repair directions are correct, but the current table is incomplete and should explicitly include runtime-capability shims, council orchestrate-session, council replay graph, and classified runtime tests.

resolvedQuestions.question: Are the Class A and Class B path-repair rules complete and directionally correct for live source files and tests?
resolvedQuestions.answer: The direction rules are correct, but the concrete phase-002 inventory should add replay-graph-from-artifacts, deep-review shims, and a classified runtime-test list.

resolvedQuestions.question: Are the forward and reverse path-coupling repair rules correct?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Yes. Forward runtime-to-workflows paths keep hop count and delete the old segment; reverse workflow-to-runtime paths remove one hop and target runtime/.

resolvedQuestions.question: Is the external reference migration plan complete across commands, agents, READMEs, hooks, advisor corpus, skill graph, generated contracts, and examples?
resolvedQuestions.answer: The child 003 plan is directionally complete across the major live surfaces; it should preserve category staging, synchronize advisor codegen/drift guards, regenerate compiled command contracts, and dedupe literal graph-edge duplicates such as cli-opencode.

resolvedQuestions.question: Is the proposed system-deep-loop target layout structurally sound?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Yes, if runtime remains infrastructure-only and exactly one graph-metadata.json is fresh-authored at the hub.

resolvedQuestions.question: Is the proposed system-deep-loop/runtime layout structurally coherent without duplicate skill identity or mode ambiguity?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Yes, if system-deep-loop remains the single public hub and runtime is demoted to backend infrastructure without routable skill metadata.

resolvedQuestions.question: Is the proposed system-deep-loop/runtime/ structural layout correct, or does it create mode/skill identity ambiguity?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Yes, if runtime is kept as non-routable infrastructure under the hub and the unified skill has exactly one graph-metadata identity.

resolvedQuestions.question: Is the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow accounted for?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Yes, and it is load-bearing test/typecheck wiring scoped into 002.

resolvedQuestions.question: Is the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow repair complete beyond package and tsconfig files?
resolvedQuestions.answer: The four planned edits are necessary, but phase 002 should also cover artifact-root, artifact-root tests, and test:council anchor fixtures; broader system-spec-kit references belong to child 003 unless their suites are run in phase 002.

resolvedQuestions.question: Is the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow repair correctly scoped to phase 002, and are there hidden borrow paths beyond the four planned files?
resolvedQuestions.answer: The four listed borrow edits are phase-002 work, but the plan should add artifact-root.cjs, artifact-root.vitest.ts, and council-playbook-anchor-integrity.vitest.ts for the planned gates.

resolvedQuestions.question: Should fallback-router.ts be wired now for GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback, or remain optional/deferred?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Keep it optional/deferred for the core merge. If the operator chooses to land it, amend child 004 so model substitution is implemented in fanout-run.cjs or through an explicit pool hook, with a fallback registry/config surface, attribution events, and a forced-failure GLM-to-MiMo integration test.

resolvedQuestions.question: Should fallback-router.ts be wired now for GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Fallback routing is useful optional hardening but not required for the core merge. Keep child 004 operator-gated unless GLM-heavy fan-out reruns need automatic substitution before closeout.

resolvedQuestions.question: What command, agent, README, graph metadata, advisor-corpus, and model-prompt references must migrate or remain stable?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Executable command YAML, generated contracts, agent helper paths, README navigation, graph metadata, and advisor corpus/parity fixtures must migrate; /deep:* command names, OpenCode agent slugs, and sk-prompt-models model IDs/profile files should remain stable.

14 relative requires (workflows->runtime) break LOUDLY (MODULE_NOT_FOUND); 50 string literals (runtime->workflows in compile-command-contracts.cjs + 2 other scripts) break SILENTLY. check-contract-drift.cjs is the built-in migration gate.

6 migration tiers by loudness. Advisor corpus highest-risk (MERGED_DEEP_SKILL_ID -> ~40 routing entries, coupled-pair atomic migration gated by drift-guard). .claude/agents mirror is silent trap. 2 automated guards bound risk; ungated prose needs grep pass.
Automatic GLM-to-MiMo fallback is not currently live.
Child 002 covers both coupling directions, tooling-borrow, metadata consolidation, and validation gates.
Child 003 covers advisor, command, docs, tests, ledgers, graph metadata, and examples with dependency ordering.
Do NOT wire fallback-router during merge. Zero callers confirmed; wiring is 4-piece feature out of scope; approval-guard rejects unapproved MiMo swaps. Preserve router, track as post-merge follow-up.
Fallback wiring remains optional unless the operator expands scope.

resolvedQuestions.text: Should fallback-router.ts be wired into real GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback now, or remain an offline/reference-only primitive for this phase?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Keep fallback-router wiring optional/operator-gated and non-blocking for the core merge. If implemented, amend child 004 with an explicit fallback registry and canonical-to-provider slug mapping, model-aware substitution in fanout-run.cjs or a generic retry-exhausted hook, attribution events/labels, and forced-failure GLM-to-MiMo integration coverage.

resolvedQuestions.text: Which system-spec-kit tooling should be borrowed, wrapped, or left external instead of duplicated inside the merged deep-loop system?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Keep spec-folder topology, validation, templates, memory/context save, and canonical resource-map extraction under system-spec-kit. Consume them through narrow deep-loop seams and rewrite external consumer configs rather than duplicating the tooling.

Mechanically safe IF nesting depth preserved. Runtime carries own node_modules/package.json/tsconfig as a unit. system-spec-kit path coupling is depth-invariant. No orphaned entry points. mode-registry.json + 3 command entry points are rename keystones.

resolvedQuestions.text: Does the proposed system-deep-loop structural layout preserve the current workflow/runtime split without creating ambiguous ownership or broken relative paths?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Preserve workflows as public hub/mode packets and runtime as internal backend under the merged system root; do not flatten ownership.

resolvedQuestions.text: Which bidirectional path couplings between deep-loop-workflows, deep-loop-runtime, commands, agents, docs, tests, and scripts must be repaired mechanically during the merge?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Rewrite command YAML and compiled contracts, doctor routes/assets, orchestrator/agent prompt contracts, advisor Python/TS/tests/metadata, system-spec-kit test/optimizer/docs, and runtime integration docs atomically with the move.

resolvedQuestions.text: What reference migration is required across commands, agents, READMEs, skill docs, feature catalogs, manual testing playbooks, specs, and advisor corpus entries?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Update skill identity, structured routing fields, advisor constants/projections/corpus labels, generated command contracts, agent mirrors, hooks/plugins/CI, graph metadata edges, and live docs/playbooks/catalogs. Freeze /deep:* commands and agent names, leave historical specs alone, and close with residual-grep plus routing accuracy baselines.

system-spec-kit coupling is NEUTRAL to merge (3 depth-invariant intentional seams). Merge is net simplification on workflows<->runtime axis (15 requires + 50 literals become intra-skill). Do NOT inline review-research-paths.cjs.

keyFindings.finding: .claude/agents mirror (5 files x 2 runtimes) is silent-migration trap; ungated prose.

keyFindings.title: 003 has the right dependency ordering and exit gates
keyFindings.summary: The external migration plan requires baseline inventory, hardcoded constants first, generated projection/codegen, command-contract regeneration, corpus re-baseline, and full exit gates.

keyFindings.title: 004 plan misses slug→registry-id model adapter (fan-out dispatches full provider slug, registry keys on bare id)

keyFindings.summary: 11+ parity invariants hardcode skill id (loud guard, must move in lockstep)

keyFindings.finding: 14 workflows->runtime requires break loudly; repair is mechanical (depth + skill-name).
50 runtime->workflows string literals in compile-command-contracts.cjs break SILENTLY — highest-risk coupling class.

keyFindings.summary: A fallback lineage needs a distinct label/artifact directory and status event to avoid mixing failed GLM retry state with MiMo replacement output.
Active system-spec-kit eval fixtures intentionally contain historical old-name queries/decoys and should be reviewed or allowlisted, not blindly renamed.

keyFindings.title: Add replay-graph-from-artifacts.cjs to internal path-repair scope

keyFindings.recommendation: Patch child 002 Stage 3a or its implementation checklist to include this script alongside other internal workflow-to-runtime path repairs.

keyFindings.title: Advisor does not read mode-registry.json at runtime — rename needs projection-map regen + 3 hardcoded-constant updates + drift-guard rerun

keyFindings.recommendation: Update Python, TypeScript, drift tests, generated projections, and corpus expectations as one advisor migration unit, followed by validation/rebaseline.

keyFindings.finding: advisor MERGED_DEEP_SKILL_ID='deep-loop-workflows' propagates to ~40 routing entries; registry + maps are coupled-pair requiring atomic migration.

keyFindings.title: Advisor migration is constants plus codegen plus corpus rebaseline
keyFindings.summary: Advisor Python/TS constants, drift guard hash inputs, routing labels, and divergence ledger need synchronized update and accuracy re-baseline.

keyFindings.title: Advisor migration is the highest-risk reference area
keyFindings.summary: The advisor has generated projection/hash surfaces, hand constants, lexical scoring boosts, graph edges, corpus, and divergence fixtures.

keyFindings.title: Advisor migration needs drift-guard plus accuracy re-baseline
keyFindings.summary: The merged deep-loop identity is encoded in Python, TypeScript aliases, mode-registry projection tests, corpus rows, and approved divergence ledger entries. Rename must preserve the projection hash and meet the pre-change routing-accuracy baseline.
Advisor migration requires constants, generated projection, drift guard, corpus, and rebaseline work.

keyFindings.title: Advisor projection is a migration target
keyFindings.summary: Python and TypeScript advisor paths hardcode the merged skill id and registry projection, so advisor source/tests/metadata must migrate with the hub.

keyFindings.title: Advisor projection migration must be synchronized
keyFindings.summary: Python, TypeScript scorer files, and the drift guard all hardcode the merged deep skill identity and registry/projection contract.

keyFindings.title: Advisor projections and ledgers are migration-critical
keyFindings.summary: The rename must cover advisor code constants, generated projections, tests, routing corpora, and approved-divergence ledgers.

keyFindings.title: Agent references must update both runtime mirrors
keyFindings.summary: `.opencode/agents` and `.claude/agents` both contain old deep-loop paths and registry references, so mirror sync is a migration requirement.

keyFindings.title: Artifact topology remains single-source via runtime seam
keyFindings.summary: `artifact-root.cjs` re-exports the system-spec-kit topology resolver so deep-loop consumers use a backend seam without copying the artifact contract.

keyFindings.title: artifact-root is a hidden system-spec-kit seam

keyFindings.title: artifact-root.cjs internal path.join + 2 test companions missed by 002 Stage-3b
artifact-root.cjs is a missed system-spec-kit path seam
keyFindings.summary: After runtime moves under system-deep-loop/runtime, artifact-root.cjs's relative require to system-spec-kit needs one more parent hop. Its test constant and dependency-seams guard also need updates or the validation surface becomes misleading.
Borrow has two natures: tooling (runtime->spec-kit) vs code (spec-kit tests->runtime)
Borrow surface ~6-8 sites, not 4

keyFindings.finding: check-contract-drift.cjs is a built-in migration-completeness gate if migrated first.

keyFindings.summary: Child 002's path plan is directionally correct but should add render-command-contract.cjs WORKSPACE_ROOT depth repair before implementation.

keyFindings.title: Class A and Class B path-direction rules are correct
keyFindings.summary: Runtime-to-workflows keeps hop count and deletes the sibling segment; workflows-to-runtime loses one hop and targets runtime/.

keyFindings.title: Closeout must remove compatibility shims and pass full migration gates
keyFindings.claim: Temporary compatibility paths are only acceptable during migration; parent success requires no old top-level skill folders and clean residual old-id grep outside allowlists.

keyFindings.title: Detached fan-out boundary conflicts with YAML spec.md mutation — guard is one-sided

Detached fan-out lineage boundary conflicts with YAML spec.md mutation (not gated on fanout_lineage_artifact_dir).

keyFindings.title: External migration is executable, not just prose

keyFindings.title: External migration plan is directionally complete and category-driven

keyFindings.title: External reference migration is genuinely broad

keyFindings.title: Fallback wiring remains optional for the core merge
keyFindings.summary: The parent and child specs mark fallback-router wiring as optional/operator-gated behavior work, not a structural merge closeout dependency.

keyFindings.title: Fallback wiring should remain optional unless scope changes
keyFindings.summary: Child 004 is explicitly optional and awaiting operator decision; it should be called out but not used to block child 002/003.

keyFindings.title: fallback-router is ready but has no fanout caller

keyFindings.title: fallback-router is shipped and validates fallback graphs
keyFindings.summary: The router validates graph safety and resolves failed models to fallback or fail-fast decisions.

keyFindings.title: fallback-router.ts is ready but unused by fan-out

keyFindings.title: fallback-router.ts is tested but effectively unwired

keyFindings.recommendation: Keep fallback-router wiring optional for the structural merge; if child 004 proceeds, let fanout-run own model fallback policy and integrate through a retry-exhausted seam.

keyFindings.title: fallback-router.ts needs an explicit caller if 004 is accepted
keyFindings.summary: The router enforces cross-pool and approved-model guards, while fanout-pool currently retries and marks retry exhaustion without resolving a fallback target.

keyFindings.finding: fallback-router.ts zero callers confirmed; dormant but valid. Wiring is 4-piece feature. Approval-guard rejects unapproved MiMo swaps.

keyFindings.title: Fanout retry exhaustion does not currently invoke fallback resolution
keyFindings.summary: `fanout-pool.cjs` retries and then settles retry-exhausted failures; the optional plan adds the missing resolveFallback call site.

keyFindings.title: fanout-pool only requeues same-label retries
keyFindings.summary: The retry branch increments retryCounts and pushes the same item index back to the queue.

keyFindings.title: Keep runtime nested as infrastructure, not a workflow mode

keyFindings.recommendation: Preserve runtime/ as an internal infrastructure subtree under system-deep-loop and keep workflow modes limited to deep-research, deep-review, deep-ai-council, and deep-improvement.

keyFindings.title: Legal convergence reached after 4 iterations
keyFindings.summary: All tracked questions are answered, minIterations is satisfied, and source diversity/quality gates pass.

keyFindings.title: Runtime should be nested infrastructure, not a workflow mode

keyFindings.summary: The migration should preserve meaningful contextual edges while collapsing literal duplicate targets.

keyFindings.title: Spec-kit governance stays external
keyFindings.summary: Spec validation, templates, generate-context continuity, and memory save remain system-spec-kit ownership; deep-loop should not duplicate them.
system-deep-loop/runtime should remain infrastructure, not a workflow mode.
System-spec-kit borrow repair needs hidden artifact-root and test:council fixture coverage beyond the four planned path edits.

keyFindings.title: system-spec-kit council test discovery must move to nested runtime paths
keyFindings.claim: system-spec-kit still references runtime council tests through old relative paths and must be updated after nesting.
keyFindings.recommendation: Repair the reverse test-discovery paths in the same structural phase as runtime nesting and keep system-spec-kit council tests in the closeout gate.

keyFindings.title: System-spec-kit is a high-risk migration consumer
keyFindings.summary: Package scripts, vitest globs, workflow docs, and reducer tests still reference old deep-loop paths.

keyFindings.finding: system-spec-kit path coupling is depth-invariant to rename: both old/new homes at skills/<skill>/lib/deep-loop/ depth.

keyFindings.title: System-spec-kit tooling-borrow repair is load-bearing
keyFindings.summary: The runtime typecheck and system-spec-kit council test paths cross the move boundary and belong in child 002 validation.

keyFindings.title: system-spec-kit verification points at both old roots
keyFindings.summary: Vitest discovery and optimizer manifests reference runtime tests and workflow configs, making system-spec-kit part of the migration surface.

keyFindings.title: Target layout is coherent if runtime stays infrastructure-only
keyFindings.summary: The proposed layout keeps four workflow packets at hub level and nests former runtime under runtime/ with no mode-registry entry.
Target layout structurally correct (1:1 with live runtime tree)
test:council include glob can silently shrink the test set; needs non-empty-match assertion.

keyFindings.title: test:council needs council-playbook-anchor-integrity path updates
keyFindings.summary: The test:council script includes council-playbook-anchor-integrity, which hardcodes deep-loop-runtime test paths and regexes.
The current model profile registry lists both GLM-5.2 and MiMo-V2.5-Pro with fallback_target null, so GLM-to-MiMo fallback would be new policy/configuration.
The system-spec-kit borrow is load-bearing and should be path-repaired, not decoupled, in child 002.
The target layout is valid only with one public skill identity and a non-routable nested runtime.

keyFindings.title: The tooling-borrow is load-bearing
keyFindings.summary: Runtime package scripts and tsconfig borrow system-spec-kit tooling, while system-spec-kit vitest includes runtime tests. Those paths must move in 002.

keyFindings.title: Tooling-borrow repair belongs in structural phase 002
keyFindings.summary: runtime and system-spec-kit point at each other for typecheck/test discovery, so this is move-time repair.
Two package.json under one skill; sanity-check package_skill --check

keyFindings.text: Runtime folds as self-contained runtime/ subtree; no orphaned entry points.
THREE+1 path-repair mechanisms: (A) absolute-string, (A') reverse absolute shell-out, (B) forward-relative segment-delete, (C) +1-hop tooling-borrow, (D) reverse-relative hop-reduce+rename.
Reverse coupling = ~12 code + 1 reverse-absolute shell-out + ~28 test-fixture strings.
REQ-003 system-spec-kit surface ~9 sites/5 files, not 4.
Tooling-borrow net neutral; node_modules borrow already shipped (stale claim).
Advisor already has merge-identity layer (aliases.ts:92-109); system-deep-loop is SECOND fold = id-rename of MERGED_DEEP_SKILL_ID.
Divergence-ledger reason strings are historical narration; mechanical replace corrupts them (REQ-007 ratchet guard).
skill-graph.json generated; hand-edit trap; edit graph-metadata.json sources + re-scan.
Today's failure path is same-model retry-and-salvage (fanout-pool.cjs), not model-swap.
DEFER fallback-router wiring: zero merge-blocking dependency, no router-contract debt.
orchestrate.md hardcodes mode-registry.json path 3x x2 agent trees = 6 critical edits.

ruledOutDirections.reason: runtime is infrastructure, not a mode.
Forward and reverse path repairs differ.
Optional feature scope needs operator decision.

ruledOutDirections.text: Mechanical full-file replace on divergence ledger.
Wire fallback-router as part of the merge.

schema_mismatch.message: Registry uses non-canonical key "findings" instead of "keyFindings"; coerced 25 entries.
Registry uses non-canonical key "findings" instead of "keyFindings"; coerced 8 entries.
